title: Cowork Vault skill pack
description: Describes an installable pack so bootstrap-vault can install it generically. Adding a vertical means adding a directory with one of these — never editing bootstrap-vault.

name.description: Directory name and install identifier, e.g. "realtor".

description.description: One or two sentences in the user's language, not the builder's.

whenToOffer.description: Signals that make this pack worth offering. bootstrap-vault matches against the conversation; it never installs a non-default pack unprompted.

default.description: true installs at INIT. Vertical packs are false — installed only on request.

surface.description: The phrases the user actually says. Keep it to about three; this is what gets presented, not the skill list.

riskGate.description: Required for any skill above L1. Surfaced before the first privileged action.

prompt.description: Shown verbatim. Plain language, honest about what could go wrong.

onDecline.description: dry-run reports what it would do and changes nothing.

sameAs.description: Reuse another skill's gate; ask once, cover both.

from.description: Source path within the pack, for files shipped verbatim.

template.description: Write a commented template for the user to fill in. Never invent data.

empty.description: Create empty — ledgers and append-only logs.

ifAbsent.description: Never overwrite; the user may have tuned it.

note.description: Guidance for the installing agent, not the user.

required.description: false means the pack degrades gracefully — say how.

why.description: Told to the user when prompting them to connect it.

schedules.description: Cadences to walk the user through via /schedule. CONFIG.md [scheduled-tasks] records intent and is NOT an executor.

docs.description: Repo-relative path to the pack's end-user guide.

extensions.description: MCP bundles (.mcpb) this pack needs. These install into Claude Desktop, NOT into the vault — a vault is data a session reads, an extension is a process Claude launches. bootstrap-vault checks for the named tools and guides the user through installing anything missing.

required.description: false means the pack degrades — say what stops working.

provides.description: Tool names it contributes. Used to detect whether it is already installed.

why.description: Told to the user when prompting them to install it.

setupDoc.description: Where the install and authorization steps live.

alternativeTo.description: What the pack falls back to when this is absent.

firstRun.description: A skill to offer the moment the pack finishes installing — the bridge between 'installed' and 'useful'. For a pack adopted mid-work, this is usually an importer that finds what already exists.

why.description: One line, in the user's terms, for why it is worth doing now.

skipIf.description: Condition under which offering it would be noise, e.g. the registry already having deals.
